texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Programme Overview
texts.text: 1. Programme Overview

texts.orig: Programme: Klang Valley East Metro Line
texts.text: Programme: Klang Valley East Metro Line

texts.text: Delivery model: Multi-package design and construction programme

texts.text: Route form: Predominantly elevated urban metro corridor

texts.text: Depot: Bandar Selatan Operations and Stabling Depot

texts.text: Baseline commencement date: 01 Jul 2025

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Baseline targeted completion date: 30 Jun 2028
texts.text: Baseline targeted completion date: 30 Jun 2028

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Klang Valley East Metro Line is a new rapid transit corridor developed to improve east-west mobility across the eastern and south-eastern urban belt of the Klang Valley. The line is intended to provide direct rail connectivity between established residential catchments, emerging commercial districts, and major interchange nodes that are currently dependent on congested road corridors.
texts.text: The Klang Valley East Metro Line is a new rapid transit corridor developed to improve east-west mobility across the eastern and south-eastern urban belt of the Klang Valley. The line is intended to provide direct rail connectivity between established residential catchments, emerging commercial districts, and major interchange nodes that are currently dependent on congested road corridors.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The programme is structured to deliver a new dedicated metro alignment, associated stations, depot and operations facilities, and the full rail systems required for safe and reliable passenger service.
texts.text: The programme is structured to deliver a new dedicated metro alignment, associated stations, depot and operations facilities, and the full rail systems required for safe and reliable passenger service.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The programme is intended to address sustained passenger growth on existing feeder corridors, recurring peak-hour congestion on parallel road routes, and the current lack of direct orbital movement between several high-demand urban catchments. In addition to transport capacity, the line is expected to support station-area redevelopment, improve first-mile and last-mile accessibility, and strengthen resilience across the wider public transport network.
texts.text: The programme is intended to address sustained passenger growth on existing feeder corridors, recurring peak-hour congestion on parallel road routes, and the current lack of direct orbital movement between several high-demand urban catchments. In addition to transport capacity, the line is expected to support station-area redevelopment, improve first-mile and last-mile accessibility, and strengthen resilience across the wider public transport network.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Strategic Purpose
texts.text: 2. Strategic Purpose

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The East Metro Line is intended to:
texts.text: The East Metro Line is intended to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. improve travel times between the north-eastern and south-eastern urban growth corridors
texts.text: improve travel times between the north-eastern and south-eastern urban growth corridors

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. reduce dependency on heavily loaded arterial roads and parallel bus corridors
texts.text: reduce dependency on heavily loaded arterial roads and parallel bus corridors

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. strengthen interchange access with existing rail and bus networks
texts.text: strengthen interchange access with existing rail and bus networks

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. support long-term transit-oriented development at selected stations and interchange nodes
texts.text: support long-term transit-oriented development at selected stations and interchange nodes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In operational terms, the line is expected to relieve demand pressure on overloaded bus corridors, improve access to established interchange stations, and provide a higher-capacity alternative to private vehicle commuting on several constrained east-west movements.
texts.text: In operational terms, the line is expected to relieve demand pressure on overloaded bus corridors, improve access to established interchange stations, and provide a higher-capacity alternative to private vehicle commuting on several constrained east-west movements.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Corridor Description
texts.text: 3. Corridor Description

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The line extends from Taman Melawati in the north-east to Bandar Selatan Gateway in the south-east. The alignment passes through mixed residential, commercial, redevelopment, and interchange districts and is planned primarily on elevated guideway to reduce land take and interface disruption.
texts.text: The line extends from Taman Melawati in the north-east to Bandar Selatan Gateway in the south-east. The alignment passes through mixed residential, commercial, redevelopment, and interchange districts and is planned primarily on elevated guideway to reduce land take and interface disruption.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Key network integration points are:
texts.text: Key network integration points are:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Setiawangsa Connect , with bus and local feeder interchange integration
texts.text: Setiawangsa Connect , with bus and local feeder interchange integration

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Pandan Gateway , with provision for interchange to an existing urban rail corridor
texts.text: Pandan Gateway , with provision for interchange to an existing urban rail corridor

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Salak Selatan Interchange , with regional rail and bus transfer connectivity
texts.text: Salak Selatan Interchange , with regional rail and bus transfer connectivity

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These interchange locations are considered critical to the business case because they convert the East Metro Line from a point-to-point corridor into a network-strengthening connection for the wider Klang Valley rail system.
texts.text: These interchange locations are considered critical to the business case because they convert the East Metro Line from a point-to-point corridor into a network-strengthening connection for the wider Klang Valley rail system.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The programme also includes:
texts.text: The programme also includes:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: the Bandar Selatan Operations and Stabling Depot
texts.text: the Bandar Selatan Operations and Stabling Depot

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: line-wide signalling, traction power, telecoms, SCADA, and control integration
texts.text: line-wide signalling, traction power, telecoms, SCADA, and control integration

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: station access, pedestrian circulation, and local transport integration works
texts.text: station access, pedestrian circulation, and local transport integration works

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: selected park-and-ride and feeder access improvements at outer-corridor stations
texts.text: selected park-and-ride and feeder access improvements at outer-corridor stations

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Station planning has been developed to balance corridor accessibility, interchange utility, and long-term development potential. Outer stations are intended to improve catchment access and feeder integration, while central interchange stations are intended to capture higher transfer volumes and support corridor-wide ridership growth.
texts.text: Station planning has been developed to balance corridor accessibility, interchange utility, and long-term development potential. Outer stations are intended to improve catchment access and feeder integration, while central interchange stations are intended to capture higher transfer volumes and support corridor-wide ridership growth.

texts.text: rail systems packages including signalling, traction power, telecoms, SCADA, and operational control integration

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Delivery Strategy
texts.text: 5. Delivery Strategy

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The East Metro Line is planned as a multi-package delivery programme to improve constructability, procurement flexibility, interface control, and schedule resilience.
texts.text: The East Metro Line is planned as a multi-package delivery programme to improve constructability, procurement flexibility, interface control, and schedule resilience.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.1 Rationale for Package Delivery
texts.text: 5.1 Rationale for Package Delivery

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The programme is divided into packages for the following reasons:
texts.text: The programme is divided into packages for the following reasons:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: corridor conditions vary materially between sections of the line
texts.text: corridor conditions vary materially between sections of the line

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: multiple civil fronts can be delivered in parallel
texts.text: multiple civil fronts can be delivered in parallel

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: systems installation requires controlled interface management with civil completion
texts.text: systems installation requires controlled interface management with civil completion

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: authority approvals, utility conflicts, and traffic staging differ by location
texts.text: authority approvals, utility conflicts, and traffic staging differ by location

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: risk can be allocated more effectively across specialist scope packages
texts.text: risk can be allocated more effectively across specialist scope packages

texts.orig: 9. Package-Level Reporting
texts.text: 9. Package-Level Reporting

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Monthly and commercial reporting under the East Metro Line is issued at package level. Package V3 is one of the principal elevated civil and interface packages and is reported separately in accordance with the programme governance structure.
